adversarial_check.description: Mandatory CI dry-run simulation beyond the 7 listed commands, entirely in a scratchpad cp-a copy (repo untouched): (0) confirmed python3+jsonschema+pyyaml already importable, matching what the workflow's pip-install step guarantees in real CI. (1) cp -a copy preserving .git (simulating actions/checkout fetch-depth:0). (2) Extracted the workflow's exact run: command programmatically ('bash tools/validate.sh'). (3) POSITIVE dry-run: ran that exact command against the clean scratch copy -- SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed, exit 0, confirming CI would pass this tree. (4) NEGATIVE dry-run: untagged one real fence-opener in standard/NDS.md within the same scratch copy, re-ran the identical extracted command -- exited non-zero with an explicit [C3] FAIL naming the file and line, proving the CI gate is load-bearing and would genuinely block a PR carrying that violation, not merely pass by construction.
